business.type_label: author / gamebook & novel writer personal site

engine.ladder_reasoning: A writer showcasing a body of published works (gamebooks, novels, solo RPGs) — the bibliography itself is the proof and the reason to buy/subscribe, so browsing the WORK is the decision.

goal.one_action: Subscribe to the author's newsletter (Boletín); secondarily click through to buy a featured book

structure.archetype_note: Author-brand bibliography site: home features individual works as cards, plus stats, WIP dashboard and newsletter; buying happens off-site (external '¡Lo quiero!' links to expedienteanunnaki.com / huge-gamebooks.com).

mapped.observed_as: Oreikhalkos / #CódiceSalomón / Portal Ouija / Nueva Tokio 2130 / Expediente Anunnaki / Solo RPG featured works
site stats: 38 books, 20 gamebooks, 3,009 IG followers, 3,783 teas

unmapped.observed: dashboard of in-progress projects with completion percentages 30-100%
unmapped.why_no_fit: A live status/progress widget of unreleased works — not proof, not a feature list, not a CTA; it is an engagement/anticipation device with no canonical section

proposed_new.name: works-catalog
proposed_new.evidence: Home is a spine of the creator's OWN works (gamebooks, novels, solo RPGs) as cards, each buying off-site via external '¡Lo quiero!' links — matches the registry works-catalog pattern (per-work redirect, not a generic product catalog).

proposed_new.name: progress-tracker
proposed_new.evidence: Dedicated dashboard showing completion percentages (30-100%) of in-progress/unreleased works — a standing progress-status widget distinct from any listed capability.

confidence.why: fetch_quality is partial; /catalogo returned 404, no forms captured, bio page mixed generic WordPress sidebar into extraction — spine and engine are clear but the primary conversion (newsletter vs off-site buy) is inferred.

gap_findings: works-catalog with per-work OFF-SITE purchase (external '¡Lo quiero!' redirects to other domains) is not expressible by closed catalog/gallery vocab.
The WIP progress-dashboard (completion % of unreleased works) has no section or capability — an anticipation/engagement device the taxonomy cannot represent.
Trilingual (ES/EN/IT) exceeds the binary 'bilingual' capability slug.
